sections.paragraph:
f. ( Deu 29:4), a shoe, a sandal, Arabic نَعْلُ Gen 14:23 Jos 5:15, etc. In transferring a domain it was customary symbolically to deliver a shoe (as in the middle ages a glove); hence the casting down a shoe upon any country was a symbol of taking possession. Psa 60:10, “upon Edom will I cast down my shoe,” i.e. I will take possession of it, I will claim it as my own (see Rosenm. Altes und Neues Morgenland, No. 483), Psa 108:10. Elsewhere a shoe thong, shoe latchet, and a pair of shoes ( Amo 2:6 8:6 ), is used for any thing of very little value.

Dual נַעֲלַיִם Am. loc. cit., and pl. נְעָלִים, once נְעָלֹות Jos 9:5.
